title: Event Taxonomy Operator Lesson
objective: Understand the operator fundamentals behind event taxonomy in data / analytics.
beginnerExplanation: Event Taxonomy becomes easier when you break it into signal, decisions, owners, and follow-through. This lesson gives you a practical structure instead of abstract advice.
deeperExplanation: At higher levels, event taxonomy is about tradeoffs. You are deciding where to focus, what to ignore, and how to keep the work legible enough for other people to trust.
keyTakeaways: Event Taxonomy improves when the operating goal is explicit.
You need evidence, not just motion, to judge whether event taxonomy is working.
Event Taxonomy should leave behind reusable assets like notes, decisions, scorecards, or systems.
example: A founder or operator uses event taxonomy to clarify what changed, what matters next, and what must happen before the next review.
commonMistakes: Treating event taxonomy like isolated activity instead of part of a larger system.
Skipping the review loop and assuming the first answer is good enough.
Letting ownership stay vague after the decision is made.
exercise: Write a one-page note explaining how you would improve event taxonomy over the next two weeks inside a real business.
